query: I'm drafting SKILL.md for the harborview team's expense-audit workflow — can you check the frontmatter description and body structure against the authoring doctrine before I move on to the next catalog entry?
claude never picks up the timesheet-rollup skill even when people literally describe the task. how do i rewrite its description so it actually fires? the team says 'weekly rollup', not 'timesheet'
the onboarding-checklist skill came out at 900 lines with a bunch of ALL CAPS MUST blocks and a README.md sitting inside the folder — trim it down the right way without losing the trigger vocabulary
write trigger-evals.json and evals.json for the new contract-redline skill in the meridian plugin — positives across formal and casual phrasings plus near-miss negatives that share keywords
how do you write a good skill description? mine all read like generic marketing copy and never win against the other installed skills
need to add a /weekly-status command to the acme plugin — what goes in the command frontmatter vs the body, and how do i keep it from duplicating what the status-report skill already covers?
verify flagged two findings on invoice-triage: the bundled template path under templates/ doesn't resolve, and step 3 ends with 'produce a summary' which isn't checkable. fix both up to standard

drafting the escalation-triage agent for the helpdesk plugin — should the routing rules go in the description or the body? i keep wanting to sketch the workflow steps up top and something tells me that's wrong

the digest gives northwind's tracker columns verbatim — Carrier, Lane, OTP %, Claims, CPM. when i author the scorecard skill do i paraphrase those or copy them exactly, and where does their xlsx template get wired in?
three of the built skills each paste the same client-comms boilerplate paragraph — restructure so that shared doctrine lives in one referenced file instead of being duplicated per skill
here's the sharepoint export from the finance team — folders of playbooks and templates. turn it all into digests so we can figure out the skill catalog later
digests are done for the acme engagement. what skills should we actually build for them? draft catalog.json and the plan review page for the champion
the build workflow crashed halfway through the catalog — resume the build→verify→revise loop from skill 4 onward and don't redo the ones that already passed
run the trigger benchmark on the brightline plugin and tell me if we're above the accuracy target yet — last round trigger-report.json showed 3 false negatives
the champion exported her notes from the review page — apply them: rename quote-builder to bid-builder, merge the two status skills, cut the calendar one, then re-verify everything
evals passed on the harborview plugin — package it into an installable marketplace and stand up the install guide and tutorials wiki for the team
write a pytest harness that scores our RAG pipeline's outputs against a golden set — needs per-query pass/fail and a summary table at the end
our new hire needs to skill up on the deployment pipeline — put together a training doc covering the release checklist and the rollback steps
generate a synthetic practice project so the acme team can try the built plugin end to end before we roll it out to everyone
review this PR that adds retry logic to the digest extractor script — mostly worried about the error handling around partial exports
